essence: David reframes the voice script writer blueprint away from strict MVC toward a Mochaccino-style team of collaborating agents (orchestrator + sub-agent fan-out), separating one-off voice creation (AI-TLDR, Happy Dave) from per-video deep research and script shaping, and gives the agent leeway to redesign the concept.

actions.text: Redesign voice script writer blueprint as a coordinated multi-agent (Mochaccino-style) pattern or propose a better concept

essence: David maps MVC onto video script generation — a design.md/voice.md style doc as the View, a ~170-video transcript corpus distilled into a tone-of-voice shape as the Data, deep-research fact sheets as the Model, and an LLM agent as the Controller that merges them into a script — and commissions deep research on the pattern.

actions.text: Deep-research the MVC pattern applied to tone-of-voice script writing, including distilling the full video transcript corpus into a voice/design document

essence: David asks for the current status of the earlier-in-the-week cross-testing work (sets of nodes and comparisons) whose format he liked but whose meaning he never fully understood.

David declines to proceed with the Hyperframes skills install because nobody has defined what the skills are or whether they are needed — decision is to document all subsystems into the brain first and only then decide what to install.

actions.text: Document all Hyperframes subsystems in the brain before deciding which skills to install

essence: David is frustrated by an opaque long-running agent research task (89 minutes, unclear what kind of research) — feels stuck, calls it a shitty experience, and asks for a recurring update loop showing what is going on.

actions.text: Set up a recurring loop giving David progress updates on long-running research tasks

essence: David directs the agent (addressed as Angela) to find the README research/commits done ~2 weeks earlier in the Kybernesis Cortex monorepo or brains folder and reverse-engineer them into a repeatable skill for writing first-class README documents for any project.

actions.text: Locate the cortex monorepo README commits and prior research, then build a repeatable first-class README-writing skill

essence: David hunts for the lost workflow/catalogue used to download AI-TLDR videos off YouTube (to repeat it for AppyDave/Happy Dave, checking the M4 Pro too), flags that a critical OMI device conversation was lost except head and tail (possibly the OpenAI embedding hookup) to be raised as an OMI support ticket, and directs ticket status updates plus KYB-84A with optional delivery review.

actions.text: Write support ticket to OMI about the lost conversation (only head and tail retained), noting the OpenAI embedding hookup as a possible cause
Track down and document the AI-TLDR YouTube video download/catalogue workflow so it can be repeated for AppyDave
Update ticket statuses and execute KYB-84A, with delivery review if appropriate

essence: David instructs that once OMI processing finishes, the long ~7am morning transcript (three main topics plus a fourth at the end) be deeply reviewed and split into three separate documentation streams without losing information, then discuss where each should live.

actions.text: Split the long morning transcript into three documentation streams and propose where to place each

essence: David hands over one more sideline project due today — build a small application for Angela from Supporting Potential, possibly run manually.

actions.text: Build the small sideline application for Angela of Supporting Potential (due same day)

essence: Heading to bed, David decides ideas B and C get recorded as documented future concepts rather than acted on now, and asks where the OMI brain stands — remaining probe fixes, more tests, or ready to release a new code version.

actions.text: Record ideas B and C as documented future concepts, not current work
